title: Implement prompt builder module (bead/prompt.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the prompt builder that constructs the full prompt sent to agents.

## Background & Rationale
The prompt builder assembles all context needed for an agent to work on a bead:
- Bead title and description
- Workspace context
- Any relevant files mentioned
- Completion criteria
- Output format instructions

## Technical Requirements

### Prompt Template
```markdown
# Task: ${BEAD_TITLE}

## Bead ID
${BEAD_ID}

## Description
${BEAD_DESCRIPTION}

## Workspace
${WORKSPACE}

## Instructions
You are working on this task in the context of the workspace above.
Complete the task as described. When finished:
- Commit your changes with a descriptive message
- Exit with code 0 on success, non-zero on failure

## Additional Context
${ADDITIONAL_CONTEXT}
```

### Implementation
```bash
# bead/prompt.sh

_needle_build_prompt() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local workspace="$2"
    
    # Get bead details
    local bead_json=$(br show "$bead_id" --json)
    local title=$(echo "$bead_json" | jq -r '.title')
    local description=$(echo "$bead_json" | jq -r '.description // ""')
    local labels=$(echo "$bead_json" | jq -r '.labels | join(", ")')
    
    # Build context
    local context=""
    
    # Add any files mentioned in description
    local mentioned_files=$(echo "$description" | grep -oE '[a-zA-Z0-9_/.-]+\.(py|js|ts|go|rs|sh|yaml|yml|json|md)' | sort -u)
    if [[ -n "$mentioned_files" ]]; then
        context+="### Mentioned Files\n"
        for file in $mentioned_files; do
            if [[ -f "$workspace/$file" ]]; then
                context+="#### $file\n\`\`\`\n$(head -100 "$workspace/$file")\n\`\`\`\n\n"
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    # Build final prompt
    cat <<PROMPT
# Task: $title

## Bead ID
$bead_id

## Description
$description

## Labels
$labels

## Workspace
$workspace

## Instructions
You are working on this task. Complete it as described.

When finished:
- Commit your changes with a descriptive message
- The commit message should reference the bead ID: $bead_id

$context
PROMPT
}

_needle_escape_prompt() {
    # Escape special characters for safe shell embedding
    local prompt="$1"
    printf '%s' "$prompt" | sed "s/'/'\\\\''/g"
}
```

### Customization Points
- Prompt template can be customized per-workspace via `.needle.yaml`
- Context gathering can be extended via hooks (pre_execute)
- File inclusion patterns configurable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Retrieves bead details from br
- [ ] Includes title, description, labels
- [ ] Includes workspace path
- [ ] Extracts and includes mentioned files
- [ ] Escapes special characters safely
- [ ] Prompt is readable and well-formatted
- [ ] Handles missing/empty description

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, bead, prompt

title: Gap analysis: Compare implementation against plan.md and create beads for missing features
description:
Review the current NEEDLE implementation against docs/plan.md. For each section in the plan:

1. **Audit existing code** - Check if the feature/component exists in src/
2. **Identify gaps** - Note missing implementations, incomplete features, or deviations from plan
3. **Create child beads** - For each gap, create a new bead with:
   - Clear title describing the missing feature
   - Description referencing the relevant plan.md section
   - Appropriate priority (P0 for core, P1 for important, P2 for nice-to-have)
   - Dependencies on prerequisite beads
4. **Apply dependencies** - Use 'br dep add' to link beads that must complete before others

Key plan sections to audit:
- CLI commands (needle init, run, status, stop, restart, attach, list, config, pulse)
- Directory structure (src/cli/, src/runner/, src/bead/, src/agent/, etc.)
- Agent adapters (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Aider)
- Watchdog system (heartbeat, monitor, recovery)
- Effort logging and metrics
- Configuration system
- Hook system

**CROSS-REPO DEPENDENCIES:**
NEEDLE and FABRIC are related projects. When you identify features that depend on the other repo:
- FABRIC workspace: /home/coder/FABRIC (prefix: bd-)
- NEEDLE workspace: /home/coder/NEEDLE (prefix: nd-)

To create a bead in the other workspace:
  br create 'Title' --type task --priority N --description 'Description'

To add cross-repo dependency (NEEDLE bead depends on FABRIC bead):
  cd /home/coder/NEEDLE && br dep add nd-XXX bd-YYY

Example: If NEEDLE's log output format must be implemented before FABRIC can parse it, create the FABRIC bead and make it depend on the NEEDLE logging bead.

Output: A complete bead dependency graph covering all unimplemented plan features, including cross-repo dependencies where applicable.

title: Implement workspace config loader (lib/workspace.sh)
description: Workspace Configuration Loader | Overview: Load and merge workspace-level .needle.yaml with global config. | Background: Workspaces can override global settings. Need to merge configs with proper precedence. | Technical Requirements: Create lib/workspace.sh with: | 1. Find .needle.yaml in workspace root | 2. Load and parse YAML | 3. Merge with global config (workspace overrides global) | 4. Cache merged config per workspace | Implementation: | load_workspace_config() { | local workspace=$1 | local ws_config="$workspace/.needle.yaml" | local global_config="$HOME/.needle/config.yaml" | if [[ \! -f "$ws_config" ]]; then | # No workspace config, use global | yq '.' "$global_config" | return | fi | # Merge: global as base, workspace overrides | yq eval-all 'select(fileIndex == 0) * select(fileIndex == 1)' "$global_config" "$ws_config" | } | get_workspace_setting() { | local workspace=$1 key=$2 default=$3 | local config=$(load_workspace_config "$workspace") | local value=$(echo "$config" | yq "$key") | [[ "$value" == 'null' || -z "$value" ]] && echo "$default" || echo "$value" | } | Acceptance Criteria: | - Loads workspace .needle.yaml if present | - Falls back to global config | - Correctly merges nested structures | - Provides default values for missing keys | Dependencies: nd-1il (shared libs) | Labels: config, workspace, phase-1

title: Implement agent adapter test suite (tests/test_adapters.sh)
description:
## Overview
Test suite for agent adapter system - YAML loading, invocation, and output parsing.

## Background & Rationale
Agent adapters bridge NEEDLE to various AI coding CLIs. Critical for multi-agent support.

## Technical Requirements

### Test Coverage

#### Adapter Loading (agent/loader.sh)
- Loads built-in adapters from config/agents/*.yaml
- Validates adapter schema
- Detects missing required fields
- Handles malformed YAML

#### Agent Invocation (agent/dispatch.sh)
- Constructs correct CLI command
- Passes prompt correctly (stdin/file/arg)
- Sets environment variables
- Captures stdout/stderr

#### Prompt Escaping (agent/escape.sh)
- Escapes shell special characters
- Handles multi-line prompts
- Preserves formatting
- Works with heredocs

#### Token Extraction (telemetry/effort.sh)
- Parses agent output for token counts
- Handles various formats (JSON, text)
- Falls back gracefully on missing data
- Calculates cost correctly

#### Built-in Adapters (config/agents/*.yaml)
- claude adapter works
- opencode adapter works
- aider adapter works
- Custom adapters loadable

### Test Approach
```bash
# Mock agent CLI
mock_claude() {
    echo "Task completed"
    echo "TOKENS: input=100 output=50" >&2
}

# Test invocation
result=
[[ "$result" == "Task completed" ]]

# Test token extraction
tokens=
[[ "$tokens" == "100,50" ]]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ test cases covering adapter system
- [ ] All built-in adapters tested
- [ ] Mock agents used (no real API calls)
- [ ] Token extraction verified
- [ ] Prompt escaping validated
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Custom adapter loading works

## Dependencies
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher - closed)
- nd-3pu (adapter loader - closed)
- nd-68r (prompt escape - closed)
- nd-3ba (built-in adapters - closed)
- nd-28p (token extraction - closed)

## Labels
testing, agents, adapters, phase-2

title: Implement PATH management module (bootstrap/paths.sh)
description:
## Overview
Ensure ~/.local/bin is in PATH for NEEDLE binary access.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE installs to ~/.local/bin but this may not be in user's PATH.
Need to detect and offer to add to shell config.

## Technical Requirements

### PATH Detection
- Check if ~/.local/bin in $PATH
- Detect user's shell (bash, zsh, fish)
- Identify shell config file (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.)

### PATH Addition
- Prompt user to add to PATH if missing
- Support non-interactive mode (--auto-path)
- Add appropriate export line to shell config
- Reload shell config or instruct user to restart

### Export Line
```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects if ~/.local/bin in PATH
- [ ] Identifies user's shell and config file
- [ ] Prompts to add PATH in interactive mode
- [ ] Adds PATH export automatically with --auto-path
- [ ] Handles multiple shells (bash, zsh, fish)
- [ ] Skips if already in PATH

## Labels
bootstrap, path, phase-2

title: Implement needle version command
description:
## Overview
Implement `needle version` to display version information for needle and all dependencies.

## Background & Rationale
Version command is essential for:
- Troubleshooting and bug reports
- Verifying successful installation
- Checking dependency versions
- Must work even without configuration (one of the few commands that skips auto-init)

## Technical Requirements

### Output Format
```
$ needle version

NEEDLE v0.1.0
  https://github.com/user/needle

Dependencies:
  tmux      3.4      ✓ installed
  jq        1.7      ✓ installed
  yq        4.40     ✓ installed
  br        0.8.0    ✓ installed

Agents:
  claude    1.0.30   ✓ available (logged in)
  opencode  0.5.0    ✓ available
  codex     -        ✗ not found
  aider     -        ✗ not found

Config: ~/.needle/config.yaml (exists)
Logs:   ~/.needle/logs/ (3 sessions)
```

### JSON Output (--json flag)
```json
{
  "needle": {"version": "0.1.0", "repo": "..."},
  "dependencies": {
    "tmux": {"version": "3.4", "status": "installed"},
    ...
  },
  "agents": {...},
  "paths": {...}
}
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_version() {
    local json_output=false
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -j|--json) json_output=true; shift ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_version_help; return 0 ;;
            *) _needle_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;
        esac
    done
    
    if $json_output; then
        _needle_version_json
    else
        _needle_version_text
    fi
}

_needle_check_dep() {
    local cmd="$1"
    if command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        "$cmd" --version 2>&1 | head -1
    else
        echo "-"
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows needle version
- [ ] Lists all dependencies with version/status
- [ ] Lists all agents with version/status
- [ ] Shows config and log paths
- [ ] Works without config file
- [ ] JSON output with --json flag
- [ ] Help text with --help flag

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, version

title: Implement needle stop command (cli/stop.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle stop` command for stopping workers.

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
```
needle stop [WORKERS...] [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    [WORKERS...]    Worker identifiers to stop

OPTIONS:
    -a, --all         Stop all running workers
    -g, --graceful    Wait for current bead to complete [default]
    -i, --immediate   Stop immediately, release current bead
    -f, --force       Kill process without cleanup
    --timeout <SECS>  Graceful shutdown timeout [default: 300]
    -h, --help        Print help
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_stop() {
    local workers=()
    local all=false
    local mode="graceful"  # graceful | immediate | force
    local timeout=300
    
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -a|--all) all=true; shift ;;
            -g|--graceful) mode="graceful"; shift ;;
            -i|--immediate) mode="immediate"; shift ;;
            -f|--force) mode="force"; shift ;;
            --timeout) timeout="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_stop_help; return 0 ;;
            -*) _needle_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;
            *) workers+=("$1"); shift ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Get target sessions
    local sessions=()
    
    if $all; then
        while IFS= read -r session; do
            sessions+=("$session")
        done < <(tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}' 2>/dev/null | grep '^needle-')
    else
        for worker in "${workers[@]}"; do
            local session
            if [[ "$worker" == needle-* ]]; then
                session="$worker"
            else
                session=$(tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}' 2>/dev/null | grep "needle-.*-$worker$" | head -1)
            fi
            [[ -n "$session" ]] && sessions+=("$session")
        done
    fi
    
    if [[ ${#sessions[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        _needle_error "No workers to stop"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Stop each session
    for session in "${sessions[@]}"; do
        case "$mode" in
            graceful)
                _needle_stop_graceful "$session" "$timeout"
                ;;
            immediate)
                _needle_stop_immediate "$session"
                ;;
            force)
                _needle_stop_force "$session"
                ;;
        esac
    done
}

_needle_stop_graceful() {
    local session="$1"
    local timeout="$2"
    
    _needle_info "Gracefully stopping $session..."
    
    # Signal shutdown
    touch "$NEEDLE_STATE/shutdown_${session##*-}"
    
    # Wait for clean exit
    local waited=0
    while tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null && ((waited < timeout)); do
        sleep 1
        ((waited++))
    done
    
    if tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null; then
        _needle_warn "Timeout reached, killing session"
        tmux kill-session -t "$session"
    fi
    
    rm -f "$NEEDLE_STATE/shutdown_${session##*-}"
    _needle_success "Stopped $session"
}

_needle_stop_immediate() {
    local session="$1"
    
    _needle_info "Immediately stopping $session..."
    
    # Get current bead from heartbeat
    local heartbeat="$NEEDLE_STATE/heartbeats/${session}.json"
    if [[ -f "$heartbeat" ]]; then
        local bead=$(jq -r '.current_bead // ""' "$heartbeat")
        if [[ -n "$bead" ]]; then
            br update "$bead" --release --reason "immediate_stop"
            _needle_info "Released bead $bead"
        fi
    fi
    
    tmux kill-session -t "$session"
    _needle_success "Stopped $session"
}

_needle_stop_force() {
    local session="$1"
    _needle_warn "Force killing $session..."
    tmux kill-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null || true
    _needle_success "Killed $session"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stops specific workers by identifier
- [ ] --all stops all workers
- [ ] --graceful waits for bead completion
- [ ] --immediate releases bead and stops
- [ ] --force kills without cleanup
- [ ] --timeout configures wait time
- [ ] Cleans up state files

## Dependencies
- nd-u74 (tmux management)
- nd-31l (bead release)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, stop

description:
## Overview
Enhance the knot strand to verify no claimable beads exist before creating a starvation alert.

## Problem
HUMAN bead nd-u13 was created as a starvation alert, but investigation found 43+ claimable beads in the workspace. This was a false positive.

## Proposed Solution
In knot.sh, before creating an alert, verify:
```bash
claimable_count=$(br ready --json 2>/dev/null | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [[ "$claimable_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
    _needle_debug "knot: skipping alert - $claimable_count beads available"
    return 1
fi
```

title: Implement telemetry event emitter (telemetry/events.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the structured telemetry event emitter that outputs JSONL events.

## Background & Rationale
All NEEDLE activity is logged as structured JSONL events for:
- Real-time monitoring
- Historical analysis
- Debugging and troubleshooting
- Cost tracking
- Performance metrics

Events go to both stdout (for immediate feedback) and log files (for persistence).

## Technical Requirements

### Event Envelope
All events share a common structure:
```json
{
  "ts": "2026-03-01T10:00:00.123Z",
  "event": "bead.claimed",
  "session": "needle-claude-anthropic-sonnet-alpha",
  "worker": {
    "runner": "claude",
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "model": "sonnet",
    "identifier": "alpha"
  },
  "data": {
    // Event-specific data
  }
}
```

### Event Categories

**Worker Events:**
- worker.started
- worker.idle
- worker.stopped
- worker.draining

**Bead Events:**
- bead.claimed
- bead.prompt_built
- bead.agent_started
- bead.agent_completed
- bead.completed
- bead.failed
- bead.released

**Strand Events:**
- strand.started
- strand.fallthrough
- strand.completed
- strand.skipped

**Hook Events:**
- hook.started
- hook.completed
- hook.failed

**Heartbeat Events:**
- heartbeat.emitted
- heartbeat.stuck_detected
- heartbeat.recovery

**Error Events:**
- error.claim_failed
- error.agent_crash
- error.timeout

### Implementation
```bash
# telemetry/events.sh

NEEDLE_SESSION="${NEEDLE_SESSION:-}"
NEEDLE_LOG_FILE="${NEEDLE_LOG_FILE:-}"

_needle_emit_event() {
    local event="$1"
    shift
    
    # Build data object from key=value args
    local data="{}"
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        local key="${1%%=*}"
        local value="${1#*=}"
        data=$(echo "$data" | jq --arg k "$key" --arg v "$value" '. + {($k): $v}')
        shift
    done
    
    # Build full event
    local json=$(jq -nc \
        --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ)" \
        --arg event "$event" \
        --arg session "$NEEDLE_SESSION" \
        --argjson worker "$(_needle_worker_json)" \
        --argjson data "$data" \
        '{ts: $ts, event: $event, session: $session, worker: $worker, data: $data}'
    )
    
    # Output to stdout and/or file
    if [[ -n "$NEEDLE_LOG_FILE" ]]; then
        echo "$json" >> "$NEEDLE_LOG_FILE"
    fi
    
    if [[ "${NEEDLE_VERBOSE:-}" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "$json"
    fi
}

_needle_worker_json() {
    jq -nc \
        --arg runner "${NEEDLE_RUNNER:-}" \
        --arg provider "${NEEDLE_PROVIDER:-}" \
        --arg model "${NEEDLE_MODEL:-}" \
        --arg identifier "${NEEDLE_IDENTIFIER:-}" \
        '{runner: $runner, provider: $provider, model: $model, identifier: $identifier}'
}
```

### Log File Management
- One file per session: `~/.needle/logs/needle-{session}.jsonl`
- Append-only during session
- Rotate on session restart

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Events include timestamp, event type, session, worker
- [ ] Event data is well-formed JSON
- [ ] Events written to log file
- [ ] Verbose mode prints to stdout
- [ ] All event types documented
- [ ] jq formatting handles special characters

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries - json.sh)

## Labels
phase-1, telemetry, logging

title: Implement concurrency limit enforcement
description:
## Overview
Implement provider/model concurrency limits to prevent overwhelming APIs.

## Background & Rationale
Different providers have different rate limits. NEEDLE must:
- Track running workers per provider/model
- Enforce max_concurrent limits
- Block or queue new workers when at limit

## Technical Requirements

### Configuration
```yaml
limits:
  providers:
    anthropic:
      max_concurrent: 5
      requests_per_minute: 60
    openai:
      max_concurrent: 3
      requests_per_minute: 60
  models:
    claude-anthropic-opus:
      max_concurrent: 2
  global_max_concurrent: 20
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_check_concurrency() {
    local runner="$1"
    local provider="$2"
    local model="$3"
    local agent="$runner-$provider-$model"
    
    # Get limits
    local provider_limit=$(_needle_config_get "limits.providers.$provider.max_concurrent" "10")
    local model_limit=$(_needle_config_get "limits.models.$agent.max_concurrent" "$provider_limit")
    local global_limit=$(_needle_config_get "limits.global_max_concurrent" "20")
    
    # Count running workers
    local provider_count=$(needle list --provider="$provider" --json | jq 'length')
    local model_count=$(needle list --agent="$agent" --json | jq 'length')
    local global_count=$(needle list --json | jq 'length')
    
    # Check limits
    if ((model_count >= model_limit)); then
        _needle_error "Model limit reached for $agent ($model_count/$model_limit)"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ((provider_count >= provider_limit)); then
        _needle_error "Provider limit reached for $provider ($provider_count/$provider_limit)"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ((global_count >= global_limit)); then
        _needle_error "Global limit reached ($global_count/$global_limit)"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tracks workers per provider
- [ ] Tracks workers per model
- [ ] Enforces provider limits
- [ ] Enforces model limits
- [ ] Enforces global limit
- [ ] Clear error messages
- [ ] --force override option

## Dependencies
- nd-28x (needle list)
- nd-qzu (needle run)

## Labels
phase-2, limits, concurrency

title: Implement agent detection module (onboarding/agents.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the agent detection module that scans PATH for available coding CLI agents.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE wraps multiple coding CLI agents. During onboarding (and via `needle agents --scan`), we need to:
- Find which agents are installed
- Check their versions
- Verify authentication status
- Provide installation instructions for missing agents

## Technical Requirements

### Supported Agents
| Runner | CLI | Detection | Auth Check |
|--------|-----|-----------|------------|
| claude | claude | `which claude` | `claude auth status` |
| opencode | opencode | `which opencode` | `opencode config show` |
| codex | codex | `which codex` | `codex auth status` |
| aider | aider | `which aider` | Check for API key in env |

### Agent Detection
```bash
declare -A NEEDLE_AGENT_CMDS=(
    [claude]="claude"
    [opencode]="opencode"
    [codex]="codex"
    [aider]="aider"
)

declare -A NEEDLE_AGENT_INSTALL=(
    [claude]="npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
    [opencode]="go install github.com/opencode-ai/opencode@latest"
    [codex]="npm install -g @openai/codex"
    [aider]="pip install aider-chat"
)

_needle_detect_agent() {
    local agent="$1"
    local cmd="${NEEDLE_AGENT_CMDS[$agent]}"
    
    if ! command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "missing"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Get version
    local version=$(_needle_agent_version "$agent")
    
    # Check auth
    local auth=$(_needle_agent_auth_status "$agent")
    
    echo "$version|$auth"
}

_needle_agent_version() {
    local agent="$1"
    case "$agent" in
        claude)   claude --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 ;;
        opencode) opencode --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 ;;
        codex)    codex --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 ;;
        aider)    aider --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 ;;
    esac
}

_needle_agent_auth_status() {
    local agent="$1"
    case "$agent" in
        claude)
            if claude auth status 2>/dev/null | grep -q "logged in"; then
                echo "authenticated"
            else
                echo "auth-required"
            fi
            ;;
        opencode)
            if opencode config show 2>/dev/null | grep -q "api_key"; then
                echo "authenticated"
            else
                echo "auth-required"
            fi
            ;;
        # ... etc
    esac
}
```

### Scan All Agents
```bash
_needle_scan_agents() {
    local found=0
    
    for agent in "${!NEEDLE_AGENT_CMDS[@]}"; do
        local result=$(_needle_detect_agent "$agent")
        
        if [[ "$result" == "missing" ]]; then
            _needle_warn "$agent not found"
            echo "      └─ Install: ${NEEDLE_AGENT_INSTALL[$agent]}"
        else
            IFS='|' read -r version auth <<< "$result"
            _needle_success "$agent $version"
            echo "      └─ Auth: $auth"
            ((found++))
        fi
    done
    
    echo ""
    echo "    $found agents ready"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects claude, opencode, codex, aider
- [ ] Reports version for each installed agent
- [ ] Checks authentication status
- [ ] Provides install instructions for missing agents
- [ ] Gracefully handles detection failures
- [ ] Returns structured data for programmatic use

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, onboarding, agents
close_reason: Superseded by nd-3ns: Agent detection module is fully implemented in src/onboarding/agents.sh. All acceptance criteria met: detects claude/opencode/codex/aider, reports versions, checks auth status, provides install instructions, handles failures gracefully, returns JSON for programmatic use.

title: Implement agent dispatcher (agent/dispatch.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the agent dispatcher that renders invoke templates and executes agents.

## Background & Rationale
The dispatcher is the core execution engine:
1. Load agent configuration
2. Render the invoke template with variables
3. Execute via bash
4. Capture output and exit code
5. Parse results for telemetry

This must handle all input methods (heredoc, stdin, file, args) correctly.

## Technical Requirements

### Template Rendering
```bash
_needle_render_invoke() {
    local template="$1"
    local workspace="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    local bead_id="$4"
    local bead_title="$5"
    
    # Replace variables
    local rendered="$template"
    rendered="${rendered//\$\{WORKSPACE\}/$workspace}"
    rendered="${rendered//\$\{BEAD_ID\}/$bead_id}"
    rendered="${rendered//\$\{BEAD_TITLE\}/$bead_title}"
    rendered="${rendered//\$\{PROMPT\}/$prompt}"
    
    echo "$rendered"
}
```

### Input Methods

**Heredoc (default for Claude)**
```bash
_needle_dispatch_heredoc() {
    local rendered="$1"
    # Template already contains heredoc, just execute
    bash -c "$rendered"
}
```

**Stdin**
```bash
_needle_dispatch_stdin() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    echo "$prompt" | bash -c "$cmd"
}
```

**File**
```bash
_needle_dispatch_file() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local file_path="$3"
    
    echo "$prompt" > "$file_path"
    bash -c "$cmd"
    rm -f "$file_path"
}
```

**Args**
```bash
_needle_dispatch_args() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local arg_flag="$3"
    
    bash -c "$cmd $arg_flag '$prompt'"
}
```

### Main Dispatcher
```bash
_needle_dispatch_agent() {
    local agent_name="$1"
    local workspace="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    local bead_id="$4"
    local bead_title="$5"
    
    # Load agent config
    _needle_load_agent "$agent_name" || return 1
    
    # Render invoke template
    local rendered=$(_needle_render_invoke \
        "${NEEDLE_AGENT[invoke]}" \
        "$workspace" \
        "$prompt" \
        "$bead_id" \
        "$bead_title"
    )
    
    # Create output capture
    local output_file=$(mktemp)
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)
    
    # Execute based on input method
    local exit_code
    case "${NEEDLE_AGENT[input_method]}" in
        heredoc)
            bash -c "$rendered" > "$output_file" 2>&1
            exit_code=$?
            ;;
        stdin)
            echo "$prompt" | bash -c "${NEEDLE_AGENT[invoke]}" > "$output_file" 2>&1
            exit_code=$?
            ;;
        # ... etc
    esac
    
    local end_time=$(date +%s%3N)
    local duration=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    # Return results
    echo "$exit_code|$duration|$output_file"
}
```

### Error Handling
- Timeout support (kill after N seconds)
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT)
- Output capture even on failure
- Exit code preservation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Template variables rendered correctly
- [ ] Heredoc input method works
- [ ] Stdin input method works
- [ ] File input method works
- [ ] Args input method works
- [ ] Output captured to file
- [ ] Exit code captured
- [ ] Duration measured in ms
- [ ] Timeout kills process cleanly
- [ ] Special characters in prompt handled

## Dependencies
- nd-3pu (agent loader)
- nd-100 (prompt builder)

## Labels
phase-1, agent, dispatch

title: Implement shared library modules (lib/*.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create basic shared utility modules that all NEEDLE components use.

## Scope (BASIC utilities only)
This bead covers foundational utilities:
- lib/constants.sh - Version, paths, NATO alphabet, defaults
- lib/output.sh - Colored output (_needle_info, _needle_error, etc.)
- lib/paths.sh - Directory creation and PATH management
- lib/json.sh - JSON/JSONL formatting utilities

NOTE: Advanced config loading with defaults merging is in nd-2mc (config loader).

## Technical Requirements

### lib/constants.sh
- NEEDLE_VERSION, NEEDLE_HOME, NEEDLE_CONFIG paths
- NATO_ALPHABET array
- Default timeout/interval values

### lib/output.sh
- _needle_info(), _needle_success(), _needle_warn(), _needle_error()
- NO_COLOR and terminal detection

### lib/paths.sh
- _needle_ensure_dirs() - Create ~/.needle structure
- _needle_add_to_path() - PATH manipulation

### lib/json.sh
- _needle_json_emit() - JSONL event formatting

## Acceptance Criteria
- All utilities source without errors
- Colored output respects NO_COLOR
- JSON output is valid
- Directory structure created correctly

## Dependencies
None - foundational

## Labels
phase-1, infrastructure, library

title: Implement worker loop: Bead execution and effort recording
description:
## Overview
Part 2 of 3: Bead execution via agent dispatcher with effort recording.

## Parent Bead
Split from nd-qft (too large - combined loop, execution, cleanup, recovery)

## Scope
Execute claimed bead via agent dispatcher. Does NOT handle cleanup/recovery.

## Technical Requirements

### Execution Flow
```bash
execute_work() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    
    # Claim bead atomically
    if ! claim_bead "$bead_id"; then
        return 1  # Claim failed, try next
    fi
    
    # Build prompt
    prompt=$(_needle_build_prompt "$bead_id")
    
    # Execute via agent dispatcher
    start_time=$(date +%s)
    output=$(_needle_dispatch_agent "$prompt")
    exit_code=$?  # Capture command exit status
    end_time=$(date +%s)
    
    # Extract effort metrics
    tokens=$(_needle_extract_tokens "$output")
    cost=$(_needle_calculate_cost "$tokens")
    duration=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    # Record effort
    record_effort "$bead_id" "$duration" "$tokens" "$cost"
    
    # Handle exit code (handled by Part 3)
    return $exit_code
}
```

### Modules Used
- nd-31l: Atomic bead claiming
- nd-100: Prompt builder
- nd-1hr: Agent dispatcher
- nd-28p: Token extraction
- nd-ysf: Cost calculation
- nd-1cz: Effort recording

### Error Handling
- Claim failures (race lost) → try different bead
- Agent failures → return error code for Part 3 to handle
- Timeout handling via agent dispatcher

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Claims bead atomically
- [x] Builds prompt via nd-100
- [x] Dispatches to agent via nd-1hr
- [x] Extracts token counts via nd-28p
- [x] Calculates cost via nd-ysf
- [x] Records effort via nd-1cz
- [x] Returns exit code to caller

## Dependencies
- nd-qft-1 (loop structure - part 1)
- nd-31l (atomic claiming - closed)
- nd-100 (prompt builder - closed)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher - closed)
- nd-28p (token extraction - closed)
- nd-ysf (cost calculation - closed)

## Next Phase
nd-qft-3 handles cleanup and recovery

title: Implement Strand 3: Mend (strands/mend.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement Strand 3 (mend) for maintenance and cleanup tasks.

## Background & Rationale
The mend strand handles housekeeping:
- Clean up orphaned claims (assigned beads whose worker died)
- Health checks on workspace state
- Prune old logs if configured

## Technical Requirements

### Maintenance Tasks
```bash
_needle_strand_mend() {
    local workspace="$1"
    
    local work_done=false
    
    # 1. Clean orphaned claims
    if _needle_mend_orphaned_claims "$workspace"; then
        work_done=true
    fi
    
    # 2. Prune old heartbeats
    if _needle_mend_old_heartbeats; then
        work_done=true
    fi
    
    # 3. Log rotation (if configured)
    if _needle_mend_logs; then
        work_done=true
    fi
    
    if $work_done; then
        echo "work_done"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1  # No work
}

_needle_mend_orphaned_claims() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local fixed=0
    
    # Get all in-progress beads
    local in_progress=$(br list --workspace="$workspace" --status in_progress --json 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ -z "$in_progress" || "$in_progress" == "[]" ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    while IFS= read -r bead; do
        local bead_id=$(echo "$bead" | jq -r '.id')
        local assignee=$(echo "$bead" | jq -r '.assignee // ""')
        
        # Check if assignee worker is still alive
        if [[ -n "$assignee" ]]; then
            local heartbeat="$NEEDLE_STATE/heartbeats/${assignee}.json"
            
            # If no heartbeat file, worker is dead
            if [[ ! -f "$heartbeat" ]]; then
                _needle_warn "Found orphaned claim: $bead_id (worker: $assignee)"
                
                br update "$bead_id" --release --reason "orphaned_claim"
                
                _needle_emit_event "mend.orphan_released" \
                    "bead_id=$bead_id" \
                    "assignee=$assignee"
                
                ((fixed++))
            fi
        fi
    done < <(echo "$in_progress" | jq -c '.[]')
    
    if ((fixed > 0)); then
        _needle_info "Released $fixed orphaned claims"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

_needle_mend_old_heartbeats() {
    local max_age=$(_needle_config_get "mend.heartbeat_max_age" "3600")  # 1 hour
    local now=$(date +%s)
    local cleaned=0
    
    for heartbeat in "$NEEDLE_STATE/heartbeats"/*.json; do
        [[ -f "$heartbeat" ]] || continue
        
        local pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$heartbeat")
        local last=$(jq -r '.last_heartbeat' "$heartbeat")
        local last_epoch=$(date -d "$last" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
        local age=$((now - last_epoch))
        
        # If process is dead and heartbeat is old, clean up
        if ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && ((age > max_age)); then
            rm -f "$heartbeat"
            ((cleaned++))
        fi
    done
    
    if ((cleaned > 0)); then
        _needle_emit_event "mend.heartbeats_cleaned" "count=$cleaned"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

_needle_mend_logs() {
    local max_files=$(_needle_config_get "mend.max_log_files" "100")
    local log_count=$(find "$NEEDLE_LOGS" -name "*.jsonl" | wc -l)
    
    if ((log_count > max_files)); then
        # Delete oldest logs
        local to_delete=$((log_count - max_files))
        find "$NEEDLE_LOGS" -name "*.jsonl" -printf '%T+ %p\n' | \
            sort | head -n "$to_delete" | cut -d' ' -f2- | \
            xargs rm -f
        
        _needle_emit_event "mend.logs_pruned" "count=$to_delete"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects orphaned claims (assigned to dead workers)
- [ ] Releases orphaned claims
- [ ] Cleans up old heartbeat files
- [ ] Prunes old log files
- [ ] Emits mend events
- [ ] Returns work_done if anything cleaned
- [ ] Does not process beads

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine)
- nd-fjz (heartbeat files)

## Labels
phase-2, strands, mend

title: Dispatch: Use tee instead of redirect for terminal output visibility
description:
Modify src/agent/dispatch.sh to use tee so agent output is visible in terminal:

Current (line 131):
  bash -c "$rendered" > "$output_file" 2>&1

Changed to:
  bash -c "$rendered" 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"

This allows:
1. Output captured to file for token extraction
2. Output displayed to terminal (processed by agent's stream parser)
3. Agent invoke template includes its own stream-parser piping

The stream parser is NOT part of NEEDLE - it's configured in the agent's invoke template:
  invoke: |
    claude --output-format stream-json ... | /path/to/stream-parser.sh

NEEDLE just needs to not swallow the output.
notes: Changed all dispatch methods to use tee instead of redirect for terminal output visibility. Updated heredoc, stdin, file, and args dispatch methods. Used PIPESTATUS to preserve exit codes through the pipeline.

title: Implement rate limiting per provider
description:
## Overview
Implement requests-per-minute rate limiting per provider.

## Technical Requirements

### Rate Limit Tracking
```bash
# State file: ~/.needle/state/rate_limits/anthropic.json
{
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "requests": [
    {"ts": "2026-03-01T10:00:00Z"},
    {"ts": "2026-03-01T10:00:30Z"},
    // ... recent requests
  ]
}
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_check_rate_limit() {
    local provider="$1"
    local limit=$(_needle_config_get "limits.providers.$provider.requests_per_minute" "60")
    
    local state_file="$NEEDLE_STATE/rate_limits/${provider}.json"
    local now=$(date +%s)
    local minute_ago=$((now - 60))
    
    # Load and filter recent requests
    local recent=0
    if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then
        recent=$(jq --arg cutoff "$minute_ago" \
            '[.requests[] | select(.ts | fromdate > ($cutoff | tonumber))] | length' \
            "$state_file")
    fi
    
    if ((recent >= limit)); then
        _needle_warn "Rate limit: $provider at $recent/$limit rpm"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

_needle_record_request() {
    local provider="$1"
    local state_file="$NEEDLE_STATE/rate_limits/${provider}.json"
    local ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then
        jq --arg ts "$ts" '.requests += [{"ts": $ts}]' "$state_file" > "$state_file.tmp"
        mv "$state_file.tmp" "$state_file"
    else
        echo "{\"provider\": \"$provider\", \"requests\": [{\"ts\": \"$ts\"}]}" > "$state_file"
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tracks requests per provider
- [ ] Respects requests_per_minute
- [ ] Sliding window (last 60 seconds)
- [ ] Blocks when limit reached
- [ ] Retries after cooldown
- [ ] State persists across restarts

## Dependencies
- nd-1ea (concurrency limits)

## Labels
phase-2, limits, rate-limiting

title: Implement configuration hot-reload
description:
## Overview
Enable NEEDLE workers to detect and reload configuration changes without restart.

## Background & Rationale
Changing config currently requires worker restart. Hot-reload improves UX for tuning concurrency, budgets, and strand settings.

## Technical Requirements

### Config Change Detection
- Watch ~/.needle/config.yaml for mtime changes
- Watch workspace .needle.yaml for changes
- Check on heartbeat interval (every 30s)
- Use file mtime comparison (no inotify dependency)

### Reload Process
- Reload config on next strand iteration
- Validate new config before applying
- Log config reload event
- Emit telemetry event

### Reloadable Settings
- worker concurrency limits
- budget limits
- strand enablement
- rate limits
- hook configuration

### Non-Reloadable Settings
- agent paths (requires restart)
- telemetry log path (requires restart)
- workspace path (requires restart)

### Implementation
```bash
# In runner/loop.sh main loop
_check_config_reload() {
    local config_file="$HOME/.needle/config.yaml"
    local ws_config="$WORKSPACE/.needle.yaml"
    
    local current_mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$config_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    
    if [[ $current_mtime -gt $CONFIG_LOADED_AT ]]; then
        _needle_info "Config changed, reloading..."
        source lib/config.sh
        _load_config
        CONFIG_LOADED_AT=$current_mtime
        _emit_event config_reloaded
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects config file changes
- [ ] Reloads on next iteration
- [ ] Validates before applying
- [ ] Logs reload events
- [ ] Documents non-reloadable settings
- [ ] Works for both global and workspace config
- [ ] No external dependencies (no inotify)

## Dependencies
- nd-2mc (config loader - closed)
- nd-qft (worker loop - open)

## Labels
config, hot-reload, phase-3

## Root Cause Analysis (FALSE POSITIVE)

**Investigation by:** claude-code-glm-5-bravo
**Date:** 2026-03-04

### Root Cause

The worker starvation alert was a FALSE POSITIVE caused by br ready including beads that are:
1. **Assigned to other workers** - 15 of 20 "ready" beads had assignee set
2. **Human-type beads** - nd-20a itself was included in the ready count

br ready --json reported 20 ready beads
Only 5 beads had no assignee
Only 4 beads were non-human type AND unassigned

### Fix Implemented

- Beads with assignee set (assigned to other workers)
- Beads with issue_type == human (not claimable by workers)

title: Implement Strand 5: Unravel (strands/unravel.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement Strand 5 (unravel) that creates alternative approaches for blocked HUMAN beads.

## Background & Rationale
When beads are blocked waiting for human input, unravel proposes alternative approaches that can make progress without the human decision.

## Technical Requirements

### Unravel Logic
```bash
_needle_strand_unravel() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    # Find HUMAN-type beads that have been waiting
    local min_wait=$(_needle_config_get "strands.unravel.min_wait_hours" "24")
    local min_wait_seconds=$((min_wait * 3600))
    
    local human_beads=$(br list --workspace="$workspace" --status blocked --type human --json 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ -z "$human_beads" || "$human_beads" == "[]" ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    local now=$(date +%s)
    
    while IFS= read -r bead; do
        local bead_id=$(echo "$bead" | jq -r '.id')
        local created=$(echo "$bead" | jq -r '.created_at')
        local created_epoch=$(date -d "$created" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
        local age=$((now - created_epoch))
        
        # Skip if not waiting long enough
        ((age < min_wait_seconds)) && continue
        
        # Check if we already created alternatives
        local existing=$(br list --workspace="$workspace" --label "for-$bead_id" --count 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
        ((existing > 0)) && continue
        
        # Build unravel prompt
        local prompt=$(_needle_build_unravel_prompt "$bead_id" "$workspace")
        
        # Run analysis
        local result=$(_needle_dispatch_agent "$agent" "$workspace" "$prompt" "$bead_id" "unravel")
        IFS='|' read -r exit_code duration output_file <<< "$result"
        
        local analysis=$(cat "$output_file")
        rm -f "$output_file"
        
        # Create alternatives
        local alternatives=$(echo "$analysis" | jq -c '.alternatives // []')
        
        while IFS= read -r alt; do
            local title=$(echo "$alt" | jq -r '.title')
            local description=$(echo "$alt" | jq -r '.description')
            local approach=$(echo "$alt" | jq -r '.approach')
            
            br create "$title" \
                --workspace="$workspace" \
                --description "$description\n\n## Approach\n$approach" \
                --priority 2 \
                --type task \
                --label "alternative" \
                --label "for-$bead_id" \
                --label "pending-human-review"
            
            _needle_emit_event "unravel.alternative_created" \
                "parent_id=$bead_id" \
                "title=$title"
        done < <(echo "$alternatives" | jq -c '.[]')
        
        echo "work_done"
        return 0
    done < <(echo "$human_beads" | jq -c '.[]')
    
    return 1
}
```

### Unravel Prompt Template
As documented in plan.md - proposes reversible alternatives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Finds HUMAN beads waiting for input
- [ ] Respects min_wait_hours
- [ ] Avoids duplicate alternatives
- [ ] Creates alternative beads
- [ ] Labels alternatives correctly
- [ ] Emits unravel events
- [ ] Opt-in only

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)

## Labels
phase-3, strands, unravel

description: Weighted Bead Selection from Queue | Overview: Select the next bead to work on using priority-weighted random selection. | Background: Multiple beads may be available. Selection should favor higher priority beads while maintaining some randomness to prevent starvation. | Technical Requirements: Create bead/select.sh with: | 1. Get claimable beads via: br ready --unassigned --json | 2. Apply priority weighting (P0=8x, P1=4x, P2=2x, P3=1x) | 3. Random selection within weighted pool | 4. Return selected bead ID | Implementation: | select_weighted() { | local candidates=$(br ready --unassigned --json) | [[ -z "$candidates" || "$candidates" == '[]' ]] && return 1 | # Build weighted array based on priority | # P0 entries appear 8x, P1 4x, P2 2x, P3 1x | local weighted=() | while read -r bead; do | local id=$(echo "$bead" | jq -r '.id') | local priority=$(echo "$bead" | jq -r '.priority // 2') | local weight=$((8 / (priority + 1))) | for ((i=0; i<weight; i++)); do | weighted+=("$id") | done | done < <(echo "$candidates" | jq -c '.[]') | # Random selection from weighted array | local idx=$((RANDOM % ${#weighted[@]})) | echo "${weighted[$idx]}" | } | Acceptance Criteria: | - Returns valid bead ID from ready queue | - Higher priority beads selected more frequently | - Handles empty queue gracefully | - Works with br CLI output format | Dependencies: nd-2g9 (CLI skeleton), nd-1il (shared libs) | Labels: bead, core, phase-1

title: Implement needle config command (cli/config.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle config` command for viewing/editing configuration.

## Technical Requirements

### Subcommands
```
needle config show      # Display current config
needle config edit      # Open in editor
needle config validate  # Validate syntax
needle config path      # Show file paths
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_config() {
    local subcommand="${1:-show}"
    shift || true
    
    case "$subcommand" in
        show) _needle_config_show "$@" ;;
        edit) _needle_config_edit "$@" ;;
        validate) _needle_config_validate "$@" ;;
        path) _needle_config_path ;;
        -h|--help) _needle_config_help ;;
        *) _needle_error "Unknown: $subcommand" ;;
    esac
}

_needle_config_show() {
    local json_output=false
    local global=false
    local workspace=false
    
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -j|--json) json_output=true; shift ;;
            --global) global=true; shift ;;
            --workspace) workspace=true; shift ;;
            *) shift ;;
        esac
    done
    
    local config_file="$NEEDLE_CONFIG"
    $workspace && config_file=".needle.yaml"
    
    if $json_output; then
        yq -o json "$config_file" 2>/dev/null
    else
        cat "$config_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "# No configuration file"
    fi
}

_needle_config_edit() {
    local editor="${EDITOR:-vi}"
    local global=false
    [[ "$1" == "--global" ]] && global=true
    
    local config_file=".needle.yaml"
    $global && config_file="$NEEDLE_CONFIG"
    
    $editor "$config_file"
}

_needle_config_validate() {
    if yq e '.' "$NEEDLE_CONFIG" &>/dev/null; then
        _needle_success "Configuration is valid"
    else
        _needle_error "Configuration has syntax errors"
        return 1
    fi
}

_needle_config_path() {
    echo "Global:    $NEEDLE_CONFIG"
    echo "Workspace: .needle.yaml"
    echo "Logs:      $NEEDLE_LOGS"
    echo "State:     $NEEDLE_STATE"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] show displays config
- [ ] show --json outputs JSON
- [ ] edit opens in EDITOR
- [ ] validate checks syntax
- [ ] path shows all paths

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (config loading)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, config

title: Implement Strand 4: Weave (strands/weave.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement Strand 4 (weave) that creates beads from documentation gaps.

## Background & Rationale
Weave analyzes documentation (ADRs, TODOs, ROADMAPs, READMEs) and identifies features or tasks mentioned in docs that are not yet tracked as beads.

## Technical Requirements

### Weave Logic
```bash
_needle_strand_weave() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    # Check frequency limit (don't run every loop)
    local last_run_file="$NEEDLE_STATE/weave_last_run"
    local frequency=$(_needle_config_get "strands.weave.frequency" "3600")  # 1 hour
    
    if [[ -f "$last_run_file" ]]; then
        local last=$(cat "$last_run_file")
        local now=$(date +%s)
        if ((now - last < frequency)); then
            return 1  # Too soon
        fi
    fi
    
    # Find documentation files
    local doc_files=""
    for pattern in "*.md" "docs/*.md" "ADR*.md" "TODO*" "ROADMAP*"; do
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            [[ -f "$file" ]] && doc_files+="$file\n"
        done < <(find "$workspace" -name "$pattern" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -20)
    done
    
    if [[ -z "$doc_files" ]]; then
        return 1  # No docs to analyze
    fi
    
    # Get current open beads (to avoid duplicates)
    local open_beads=$(br list --workspace="$workspace" --status open --json 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Build weave prompt
    local prompt=$(_needle_build_weave_prompt "$workspace" "$doc_files" "$open_beads")
    
    # Run analysis
    local result=$(_needle_dispatch_agent "$agent" "$workspace" "$prompt" "weave" "weave-analysis")
    IFS='|' read -r exit_code duration output_file <<< "$result"
    
    local analysis=$(cat "$output_file")
    rm -f "$output_file"
    
    # Parse gaps
    local gaps=$(echo "$analysis" | jq -c '.gaps // []')
    local max_beads=$(_needle_config_get "strands.weave.max_beads_per_run" "5")
    local created=0
    
    while IFS= read -r gap && ((created < max_beads)); do
        local title=$(echo "$gap" | jq -r '.title')
        local description=$(echo "$gap" | jq -r '.description')
        local priority=$(echo "$gap" | jq -r '.priority // 2')
        local source=$(echo "$gap" | jq -r '.source_file // ""')
        
        # Create bead
        br create "$title" \
            --workspace="$workspace" \
            --description "$description" \
            --priority "$priority" \
            --type task \
            --label "weave-generated" \
            --label "from-docs"
        
        _needle_emit_event "weave.bead_created" \
            "title=$title" \
            "source=$source"
        
        ((created++))
    done < <(echo "$gaps" | jq -c '.[]')
    
    # Update last run
    date +%s > "$last_run_file"
    
    if ((created > 0)); then
        _needle_emit_event "strand.weave.completed" \
            "beads_created=$created"
        echo "work_done"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}
```

### Weave Prompt Template
As documented in plan.md - analyzes docs and returns JSON with gaps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Finds documentation files
- [ ] Respects frequency limit
- [ ] Analyzes docs for gaps
- [ ] Avoids duplicate beads
- [ ] Creates gap beads with source
- [ ] Respects max_beads_per_run
- [ ] Emits weave events
- [ ] Opt-in only (disabled by default)

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)

## Labels
phase-3, strands, weave

description: Rollback to Previous NEEDLE Version | Overview: Restore a previously installed version from backup cache. | Background: If an upgrade causes issues, users need to quickly restore a working version. | Technical Requirements: Create cli/rollback.sh with: | 1. Parse --version flag (specific version) or use most recent backup | 2. List available backups from ~/.needle/cache/*.bak | 3. Validate backup exists and is executable | 4. Swap binaries atomically (same as upgrade) | 5. Verify rollback succeeded | Usage: | needle rollback # Rollback to previous | needle rollback --version=1.2.0 # Specific version | needle rollback --list # Show available versions | Implementation: | rollback_needle() { | local target_version=$1 | local backups_dir="$HOME/.needle/cache" | if [[ -z "$target_version" ]]; then | # Find most recent backup | target_version=$(ls -t "$backups_dir"/needle-*.bak 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/.*needle-\(.*\).bak/\1/') | fi | local backup_file="$backups_dir/needle-$target_version.bak" | [[ \! -f "$backup_file" ]] && echo "No backup found for $target_version" && return 1 | # Atomic swap | mv "$HOME/.local/bin/needle" "$backups_dir/needle-$(needle --version).bak" 2>/dev/null | mv "$backup_file" "$HOME/.local/bin/needle" | echo "Rolled back to v$target_version" | } | Acceptance Criteria: | - Restores previous version from backup | - Supports specific version targeting | - Lists available backup versions | - Creates backup of current before rollback | Dependencies: nd-3ma (upgrade command), nd-1il (shared libs) | Labels: cli, upgrade, phase-2

description:
## Overview
Extract token counts from agent output for cost tracking.

## Technical Requirements

### Agent Output Patterns
```yaml
# Claude Code (JSON output)
output:
  format: json
  # Extracts from: {"input_tokens": 1234, "output_tokens": 567}

# Other agents (text patterns)
output:
  token_pattern: "tokens_used: (\\d+)"
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_extract_tokens() {
    local agent="$1"
    local output_file="$2"
    
    local format=$(_needle_agent_config "$agent" "output.format")
    local pattern=$(_needle_agent_config "$agent" "output.token_pattern")
    
    local input_tokens=0
    local output_tokens=0
    
    case "$format" in
        json)
            # Parse JSON output
            input_tokens=$(jq -r '.input_tokens // .usage.input_tokens // 0' "$output_file" 2>/dev/null)
            output_tokens=$(jq -r '.output_tokens // .usage.output_tokens // 0' "$output_file" 2>/dev/null)
            ;;
        text)
            if [[ -n "$pattern" ]]; then
                # Extract via regex
                local match=$(grep -oE "$pattern" "$output_file" | head -1)
                input_tokens=$(echo "$match" | grep -oE '[0-9]+')
            fi
            ;;
    esac
    
    echo "$input_tokens|$output_tokens"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extracts from JSON output
- [ ] Extracts via regex pattern
- [ ] Handles missing token info
- [ ] Returns input and output tokens
- [ ] Works with all agent formats

## Dependencies
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)

## Labels
phase-2, telemetry, tokens

title: Implement needle list command (cli/list.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle list` command that shows running workers.

## Background & Rationale
Users need to see what workers are currently running, their status, and what they're working on. This is essential for:
- Monitoring worker activity
- Debugging issues
- Deciding which worker to attach to

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
```
needle list [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -a, --all              Include stopped/crashed workers
    -j, --json             Output as JSON
    -w, --wide             Show extended information
    --runner <NAME>        Filter by runner
    --provider <NAME>      Filter by provider
    --model <NAME>         Filter by model
    --workspace <PATH>     Filter by workspace
    -h, --help             Print help
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_list() {
    local show_all=false
    local json_output=false
    local wide=false
    local filter_runner=""
    local filter_provider=""
    local filter_model=""
    local filter_workspace=""
    
    # Parse options...
    
    # Get tmux sessions
    local sessions=$(_needle_list_sessions)
    
    if [[ -z "$sessions" ]]; then
        if ! $json_output; then
            _needle_info "No workers running"
        else
            echo "[]"
        fi
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Get heartbeat data
    local workers=()
    
    while IFS='|' read -r session created attached; do
        # Parse session name
        # needle-{runner}-{provider}-{model}-{identifier}
        local parts=(${session//-/ })
        local runner="${parts[1]}"
        local provider="${parts[2]}"
        local model="${parts[3]}"
        local identifier="${parts[4]}"
        
        # Apply filters
        [[ -n "$filter_runner" && "$runner" != "$filter_runner" ]] && continue
        [[ -n "$filter_provider" && "$provider" != "$filter_provider" ]] && continue
        [[ -n "$filter_model" && "$model" != "$filter_model" ]] && continue
        
        # Get heartbeat info
        local heartbeat_file="$NEEDLE_STATE/heartbeats/${session}.json"
        local status="unknown"
        local current_bead=""
        local bead_duration=""
        
        if [[ -f "$heartbeat_file" ]]; then
            status=$(jq -r '.status' "$heartbeat_file")
            current_bead=$(jq -r '.current_bead // ""' "$heartbeat_file")
            local bead_started=$(jq -r '.bead_started // ""' "$heartbeat_file")
            if [[ -n "$bead_started" ]]; then
                # Calculate duration...
            fi
        fi
        
        # Get workspace from heartbeat or session
        local workspace=$(jq -r '.workspace // ""' "$heartbeat_file" 2>/dev/null)
        
        # Apply workspace filter
        [[ -n "$filter_workspace" && "$workspace" != "$filter_workspace" ]] && continue
        
        # Build worker info
        workers+=("$session|$status|$current_bead|$bead_duration|$workspace|$identifier")
    done <<< "$sessions"
    
    # Output
    if $json_output; then
        _needle_list_json "${workers[@]}"
    else
        _needle_list_table "${workers[@]}"
    fi
}

_needle_list_table() {
    printf "%-45s %-10s %-12s %-10s %s\n" "WORKER" "STATUS" "BEAD" "DURATION" "WORKSPACE"
    printf "%-45s %-10s %-12s %-10s %s\n" "------" "------" "----" "--------" "---------"
    
    for worker in "$@"; do
        IFS='|' read -r session status bead duration workspace identifier <<< "$worker"
        printf "%-45s %-10s %-12s %-10s %s\n" \
            "$session" \
            "$status" \
            "${bead:-"(idle)"}" \
            "${duration:-"-"}" \
            "$workspace"
    done
}
```

### Output Format
```
$ needle list

WORKER                                        STATUS     BEAD         DURATION   WORKSPACE
------                                        ------     ----         --------   ---------
needle-claude-anthropic-sonnet-alpha          executing  bd-123       5m 23s     /home/coder/project
needle-claude-anthropic-sonnet-bravo          idle       (idle)       -          /home/coder/project
needle-opencode-alibaba-qwen-charlie          executing  bd-456       12m 05s    /home/coder/other
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lists all running workers
- [ ] Shows session name, status, current bead
- [ ] Shows duration on current bead
- [ ] Shows workspace
- [ ] --json outputs JSON array
- [ ] --wide shows extended info
- [ ] Filters by runner/provider/model/workspace
- [ ] --all includes stopped workers
- [ ] Empty state handled gracefully

## Dependencies
- nd-u74 (tmux management)
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, list

title: Implement needle attach command (cli/attach.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle attach` command for attaching to worker tmux sessions.

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
```
needle attach [WORKER] [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    [WORKER]    Worker identifier or full session name

OPTIONS:
    -r, --read-only    Attach in read-only mode
    -l, --last         Attach to most recent worker
    -h, --help         Print help
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_attach() {
    local worker=""
    local read_only=false
    local last=false
    
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -r|--read-only) read_only=true; shift ;;
            -l|--last) last=true; shift ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_attach_help; return 0 ;;
            -*) _needle_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;
            *) worker="$1"; shift ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Get session name
    local session=""
    
    if $last || [[ -z "$worker" ]]; then
        # Get most recent session
        session=$(tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}' 2>/dev/null | grep '^needle-' | tail -1)
    elif [[ "$worker" == needle-* ]]; then
        # Full session name
        session="$worker"
    else
        # Partial match (identifier)
        session=$(tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}' 2>/dev/null | grep "^needle-.*-$worker$" | head -1)
        
        if [[ -z "$session" ]]; then
            # Try looser match
            session=$(tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}' 2>/dev/null | grep "needle-.*$worker" | head -1)
        fi
    fi
    
    if [[ -z "$session" ]]; then
        _needle_error "Worker not found: ${worker:-"(none running)"}"
        _needle_info "Run 'needle list' to see available workers"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Attach
    _needle_info "Attaching to $session..."
    _needle_info "Detach with Ctrl+B, D"
    
    _needle_attach_session "$session" "$read_only"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Attaches by identifier (alpha, bravo)
- [ ] Attaches by full session name
- [ ] --last attaches to most recent
- [ ] --read-only prevents input
- [ ] Error if worker not found
- [ ] Shows detach instructions

## Dependencies
- nd-u74 (tmux management)

phase-1, cli, attach

title: Implement billing model profiles
description:
## Overview
Implement billing model behavior profiles that adjust strand execution and priority handling.

## Background & Rationale
Different billing models (pay_per_token, use_or_lose, unlimited) should adjust NEEDLE's behavior to optimize cost vs throughput.

## Technical Requirements

### Billing Models

#### pay_per_token (Default)
- Conservative: minimize token usage
- Higher priority thresholds
- Fewer speculative strands
- Budget enforcement strict

#### use_or_lose
- Aggressive: use allocated budget
- Lower priority thresholds
- Enable all strands including opt-in
- Budget as target not limit

#### unlimited
- Maximum throughput
- All strands enabled by default
- No budget enforcement
- Lowest priority thresholds

### Configuration
```yaml
billing:
  model: pay_per_token  # pay_per_token | use_or_lose | unlimited
  daily_budget_usd: 10.00
  
strands:
  strand_4_weave: auto  # auto = follows billing model
  strand_5_unravel: auto
  strand_6_pulse: auto
```

### Behavior Adjustments
- Strand enablement based on model
- Priority threshold adjustments
- Budget enforcement strategy
- Worker concurrency defaults

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Three billing models implemented
- [ ] Strand behavior adjusts per model
- [ ] Priority thresholds vary by model
- [ ] Budget enforcement matches model
- [ ] Config schema documented
- [ ] Default is pay_per_token

## Dependencies
- nd-23s (budget enforcement - closed)
- nd-hpj (strand engine - closed)

## Labels
billing, optimization, phase-3

title: Create NEEDLE CLI skeleton with subcommand routing
description:
## Overview
Create the main `needle` CLI entry point as a self-contained bash script that routes to subcommands.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE is distributed as a single bash script (~50-100KB) that embeds all functionality. This approach ensures:
- Zero external dependencies for the core binary
- Easy distribution via curl one-liner
- Portable across Linux/macOS without compilation
- Simple updates via binary replacement

## Technical Requirements

### File Structure
```
bin/needle           # Main entry point (~500 lines)
src/cli/*.sh         # Subcommand implementations
src/lib/*.sh         # Shared utilities
```

### Main Entry Point (`bin/needle`)
1. Shebang and version declaration
2. Auto-initialization check (redirect to init if unconfigured)
3. Global option parsing (-h, --help, -v, --verbose, -q, --quiet, --no-color)
4. Subcommand routing via case statement
5. Help text generation

### Subcommand Structure
Each subcommand should be a separate function or sourced file:
- `_needle_init()` → needle init
- `_needle_run()` → needle run
- `_needle_list()` → needle list
- etc.

### Implementation Pattern
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

NEEDLE_VERSION="0.1.0"
NEEDLE_HOME="${NEEDLE_HOME:-$HOME/.needle}"

# Source libraries
source "${BASH_SOURCE%/*}/../src/lib/constants.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE%/*}/../src/lib/config.sh"

# Auto-init check (skip for init, version, help)
_needle_maybe_init() {
    case "${1:-}" in
        init|version|help|--help|-h|--version|-V) return ;;
    esac
    [[ -f "$NEEDLE_HOME/config.yaml" ]] || exec "$0" init "$@"
}

# Main routing
main() {
    _needle_maybe_init "$@"
    
    case "${1:-}" in
        init)      shift; _needle_init "$@" ;;
        run)       shift; _needle_run "$@" ;;
        list)      shift; _needle_list "$@" ;;
        # ... etc
        *)         _needle_help; exit 1 ;;
    esac
}

main "$@"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `needle` with no args shows help
- [ ] `needle --help` shows detailed help with all subcommands
- [ ] `needle version` works even without config
- [ ] `needle <unknown>` shows error and help
- [ ] Exit codes follow conventions (0=success, 1=error, 2=usage)
- [ ] Colored output with --no-color support
- [ ] Verbose mode with -v flag

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundational task.

## Labels
phase-1, infrastructure, cli

title: Implement Strand 1: Pluck (strands/pluck.sh)
description: Strand 1: Pluck - Process Beads from Queue | Overview: Core strand that claims and executes beads from the configured workspaces. | Background: This is the primary work-doing strand. Selects a bead, claims it atomically, runs mitosis check, executes via agent, and marks complete. | Technical Requirements: Create strands/pluck.sh with: | 1. Get configured workspaces from config | 2. For each workspace, attempt to claim a bead | 3. Run mitosis check on claimed bead | 4. If mitosis triggered, split and release | 5. If atomic, build prompt and dispatch to agent | 6. Capture output and exit code | 7. Mark bead complete or failed | 8. Emit telemetry events throughout | Implementation: | strand_pluck() { | _emit_event 'strand.started' '{"strand":1,"name":"pluck"}' | local workspaces=$(get_workspaces) | for ws in $workspaces; do | cd "$ws" || continue | local bead_id=$(claim_bead "$NEEDLE_WORKER") | [[ -z "$bead_id" ]] && continue | _emit_event 'bead.claimed' "{\"bead_id\":\"$bead_id\"}" | # Check mitosis | if should_split "$bead_id"; then | perform_mitosis "$bead_id" | release_bead "$bead_id" | continue | fi | # Execute | local prompt=$(build_prompt "$bead_id") | local output exit_code | output=$(dispatch_agent "$NEEDLE_AGENT" "$prompt") | exit_code=$? | # Complete or fail | if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then | br update "$bead_id" --status closed | _emit_event 'bead.completed' | else | br update "$bead_id" --status blocked --label failed | _emit_event 'bead.failed' | fi | return 0 # Work found | done | return 1 # No work | } | Acceptance Criteria: | - Claims beads atomically | - Integrates with mitosis system | - Dispatches to configured agent | - Correctly marks completion/failure | Dependencies: nd-31l (claim), nd-23h (select), nd-39h (mitosis), nd-100 (prompt), nd-1hr (dispatch) | Labels: strand, core, phase-1

comments.author: Jed Arden
comments.text:
# Root Cause Analysis - nd-2j8

## FALSE POSITIVE Starvation Alert

This was a **FALSE POSITIVE** starvation alert.

### The Bug

The `_needle_get_claimable_beads()` function in `src/bead/select.sh` was missing the HUMAN type filter when `br ready` succeeded.

**Before:**
- When `br ready` returned a valid JSON array, it was returned immediately
- HUMAN type beads (alerts) were included in results
- The filter to exclude HUMAN types only existed in the fallback path

**Impact:**
- Workers would see the HUMAN alert bead as "claimable"
- This could cause confusion in the claim/selection logic
- The alert was created because the worker thought there was no work

### The Fix

Added HUMAN type filter to the `br ready` success path in `src/bead/select.sh` (lines 148-177).

```bash
# FIX: Filter out HUMAN type beads (alerts, not work items)
filtered_candidates=$(echo "$candidates" | jq -c '
    [.[] | select(
        .issue_type == null or .issue_type != "human"
    )]
' 2>/dev/null)
```

### Verification

After fix, the function correctly returns 4 claimable beads:
- nd-2q6 (P2): Implement needle init: State files
- nd-21h (P3): Implement Pulse detector: Security scan
- nd-1fr (P3): Implement Pulse detector: Dependency freshness
- nd-gn2 (P3): Implement Pulse detectors: Doc drift

### Related Beads
- nd-1ak: Improve starvation alert false positive detection
- nd-1xl: Improve starvation alert verification before creating HUMAN bead

---
*Fixed by claude-code-glm-5-bravo alternative exploration*

title: Implement bead selection test suite (tests/test_priority.sh)
description:
## Overview
Test suite for bead selection weighted priority algorithm (bead/select.sh).

## Background & Rationale
Priority algorithm determines which bead gets claimed - critical for optimal work distribution.

## Technical Requirements

### Test Coverage

#### Priority Calculations
- P0 beads always selected first
- P1 beads after P0 exhausted
- P2/P3 beads selected appropriately
- Priority boost for blocked beads

#### Weighted Selection
- Random selection within same priority
- Age factor (older beads get boost)
- Dependency factor (blockers get boost)
- Label modifiers (urgent, blocked)

#### Edge Cases
- All beads same priority
- No claimable beads
- Single bead available
- Tie-breaking logic

### Test Data

```bash
# Mock beads with various priorities
create_test_bead P0 "urgent-bug"
create_test_bead P1 "feature-1" age=5d
create_test_bead P1 "feature-2" age=1d

```

title: Implement config global loader (lib/config.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the NEEDLE configuration system with defaults merging, validation, and caching.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE uses layered configuration:
1. Built-in defaults (hardcoded)
2. Global config (~/.needle/config.yaml)
3. Workspace config (.needle.yaml)
4. CLI flags (highest priority)

This bead implements the config loading with proper merging.

## Technical Requirements

### Core Functions
```bash
# Load and cache config with defaults
load_config() {
    [[ -n "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_CACHE" ]] && echo "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_CACHE" && return
    local defaults='{
        "limits": {"global_max_concurrent": 20},
        "runner": {"polling_interval": "2s", "idle_timeout": "300s"},
        "strands": {"pluck": true, "explore": true, "mend": true},
        "effort": {"budget": {"daily_limit_usd": 50}}
    }'
    if [[ -f "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
        NEEDLE_CONFIG_CACHE=$(yq eval-all 'select(fileIndex==0) * select(fileIndex==1)' <(echo "$defaults") "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_FILE")
    else
        NEEDLE_CONFIG_CACHE="$defaults"
    fi
    echo "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_CACHE"
}

# Get config value with default fallback
get_config() {
    local key=$1 default=$2
    local value=$(load_config | yq "$key")
    [[ "$value" == 'null' || -z "$value" ]] && echo "$default" || echo "$value"
}

# Check if config exists and is valid
config_exists() {
    [[ -f "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_FILE" ]] && [[ -s "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_FILE" ]]
}
```

### Default Config Generation
```bash
create_default_config() {
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_FILE")"
    cat > "$NEEDLE_CONFIG_FILE" << 'EOF'
# NEEDLE Configuration
# See: https://github.com/user/needle#configuration

limits:
  global_max_concurrent: 20
  providers:
    anthropic:
      max_concurrent: 5

runner:
  polling_interval: 2s
  idle_timeout: 300s

strands:
  pluck: true
  explore: true
  mend: true
  weave: false
  unravel: false
  pulse: false
  knot: true

effort:
  budget:
    daily_limit_usd: 50.0
    warning_threshold: 0.8
EOF
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loads config with defaults merged
- Caches for performance (no re-parsing)
- Creates default config when missing
- Validates config structure
- Provides get_config() accessor

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (basic utilities)

## Labels
phase-1, config, infrastructure

description: OS and Package Manager Detection | Overview: Detect operating system and available package manager for dependency installation. | Background: NEEDLE needs to install deps differently on macOS (brew) vs various Linux distros (apt/dnf/pacman). | Technical Requirements: Create bootstrap/detect_os.sh with: | 1. Detect OS: Linux, macOS, WSL | 2. Detect Linux distro: Debian/Ubuntu, Fedora/RHEL, Arch | 3. Detect package manager: apt, dnf, yum, pacman, brew | 4. Provide fallback to manual binary download | Implementation: | detect_os() { | case "$(uname -s)" in | Linux*) echo 'linux' ;; | Darwin*) echo 'macos' ;; | MINGW*|CYGWIN*) echo 'windows' ;; | *) echo 'unknown' ;; | esac | } | detect_pkg_manager() { | command -v brew &>/dev/null && echo 'brew' && return | command -v apt-get &>/dev/null && echo 'apt' && return | command -v dnf &>/dev/null && echo 'dnf' && return | command -v yum &>/dev/null && echo 'yum' && return | command -v pacman &>/dev/null && echo 'pacman' && return | echo 'manual' | } | detect_arch() { | case "$(uname -m)" in | x86_64|amd64) echo 'amd64' ;; | aarch64|arm64) echo 'arm64' ;; | *) echo 'unknown' ;; | esac | } | Acceptance Criteria: | - Correctly identifies macOS vs Linux | - Identifies major Linux distros | - Detects available package manager | - Determines CPU architecture for binary downloads | Dependencies: nd-1il (shared libs) | Labels: bootstrap, detection, phase-1

title: Implement Strand 6 Pulse: Framework and state management
description:
## Overview
Part 1 of 4: Pulse strand framework with frequency checking and state management.

## Parent Bead
Split from nd-qpj (too large - combined framework + 5 detectors)

## Scope
Core Pulse framework ONLY. Does NOT implement specific detectors.

## Technical Requirements

### Frequency Checking
```bash
# strands/pulse.sh

_pulse_should_run() {
    local freq=$(get_config 'strands.pulse.frequency' '24h')
    local last_scan=$(get_pulse_state 'last_scan')
    local now=$(date +%s)
    
    [[ -z "$last_scan" || $((now - last_scan)) -gt $(parse_duration "$freq") ]]
}
```

### State Management
Files: ~/.needle/state/pulse/
- last_scan.json - Last scan timestamp
- seen_issues.json - Dedupe fingerprints

### Bead Generation
```bash
_pulse_create_bead() {
    local category="$1"
    local title="$2"
    local description="$3"
    local fingerprint="$4"
    
    # Check if already seen
    if _pulse_already_seen "$fingerprint"; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Create bead
    br create "$title" --type task --priority P2         --labels "pulse,$category,automated"         --description "$description"
    
    # Mark as seen
    _pulse_mark_seen "$fingerprint"
}
```

### Max Beads Enforcement
- Config: strands.pulse.max_beads_per_run (default: 5)
- Stop creating beads after limit reached
- Prioritize high-severity issues first

### Detector Integration
- Call detector functions (implemented in Parts 2-4)
- Collect issues from all detectors
- Sort by severity
- Create beads up to max_beads_per_run

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Respects frequency configuration
- [ ] Maintains state files correctly
- [ ] Deduplicates issues via fingerprinting
- [ ] Enforces max_beads_per_run limit
- [ ] Provides create_bead helper for detectors
- [ ] Logs pulse execution events

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine - closed)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher - closed)

## Next Phases
- nd-qpj-2: Security scan detector
- nd-qpj-3: Dependency freshness detector  
- nd-qpj-4: Doc drift and coverage detectors

title: Create one-liner installer script (install.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the install.sh script for one-liner installation.

## Background & Rationale
The one-liner install is critical for adoption:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://needle.dev/install | bash
```

This must be robust, secure, and provide a great first impression.

## Technical Requirements

### Script Structure
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

info() { echo -e "${BLUE}ℹ${NC} $*"; }
success() { echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} $*"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} $*" >&2; }

# Configuration
NEEDLE_VERSION="${NEEDLE_VERSION:-latest}"
NEEDLE_REPO="https://github.com/user/needle"
INSTALL_DIR="${NEEDLE_INSTALL_DIR:-$HOME/.local/bin}"

main() {
    info "Installing NEEDLE..."
    
    # Detect OS and architecture
    local os=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    local arch=$(uname -m)
    
    case "$arch" in
        x86_64) arch="amd64" ;;
        aarch64|arm64) arch="arm64" ;;
        *) error "Unsupported architecture: $arch"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
    
    # Get download URL
    local url
    if [[ "$NEEDLE_VERSION" == "latest" ]]; then
        url="$NEEDLE_REPO/releases/latest/download/needle-${os}-${arch}"
    else
        url="$NEEDLE_REPO/releases/download/v${NEEDLE_VERSION}/needle-${os}-${arch}"
    fi
    
    # Create install directory
    mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
    
    # Download
    info "Downloading from $url..."
    if ! curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$INSTALL_DIR/needle"; then
        error "Download failed"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    chmod +x "$INSTALL_DIR/needle"
    success "Downloaded needle to $INSTALL_DIR/needle"
    
    # Add to PATH if needed
    if [[ ":$PATH:" != *":$INSTALL_DIR:"* ]]; then
        info "Adding $INSTALL_DIR to PATH..."
        
        local shell_rc=""
        case "$SHELL" in
            */bash) shell_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
            */zsh)  shell_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
        esac
        
        if [[ -n "$shell_rc" ]]; then
            echo "export PATH=\"$INSTALL_DIR:\$PATH\"" >> "$shell_rc"
            success "Added to $shell_rc"
            info "Run 'source $shell_rc' or restart your shell"
        fi
    fi
    
    success "NEEDLE installed successfully!"
    echo ""
    info "Run 'needle init' to complete setup"
    
    # Optionally run init
    if [[ "${NEEDLE_AUTO_INIT:-}" == "true" ]]; then
        export PATH="$INSTALL_DIR:$PATH"
        exec "$INSTALL_DIR/needle" init
    fi
}

main "$@"
```

### Features
- Detects OS (Linux, macOS)
- Detects architecture (amd64, arm64)
- Downloads from GitHub releases
- Installs to ~/.local/bin
- Updates shell rc file
- Option to auto-run init

### Non-Interactive Mode
```bash
curl -fsSL https://needle.dev/install | bash -s -- --non-interactive
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Linux (Debian, RedHat, Arch)
- [ ] Works on macOS (Intel, Apple Silicon)
- [ ] Downloads correct binary for platform
- [ ] Handles download failures gracefully
- [ ] Updates PATH correctly
- [ ] Works with bash and zsh
- [ ] --non-interactive mode works
- [ ] Shows clear success message
- [ ] Prompts to run needle init

## Dependencies
None (standalone installer)

## Labels
phase-1, installer, distribution
